# Compute to Grid

> Interactive 3D educational explainer about AI data center infrastructure, workloads, and agentic AI systems.

## Canonical URL

- [Production site](https://dandanstop.me/datacenter-3d)

## What this project is

Compute to Grid is a multilingual interactive website that explains AI infrastructure as a system, not just a GPU demand story.

It is designed for:

- investors and industry analysts
- semiconductor and AI infrastructure professionals
- cloud and data center teams
- advanced readers curious about how AI compute changes power, cooling, networking, and operations

## Core chapters

1. Compute to Grid
   - power
   - cooling
   - compute and AI chips
   - networking
   - campus buildout
   - cloud and AI operations

2. One AI System, Two Workload Modes
   - training AI
   - inference AI
   - CPU coordination

3. From Response to Action
   - agentic AI workflows
   - planning
   - tool use
   - memory
   - orchestration infrastructure

## Editorial approach

- educational, not investment advice
- English-first interface with Traditional Chinese, Korean, and Japanese support
- infrastructure and systems thinking over hype cycles
- real company examples used for orientation, not stock recommendations
- future-facing interpretation through signals, constraints, and operating principles

## Useful files

- [Requirements](./REQUIREMENTS.md)
- [Changelog](./CHANGELOG.md)
- [Releases](./RELEASES.md)

- [Chapter 2 audio transcripts](./docs/chapter2-audio-transcripts.md)
- [Chapter 3 audio transcripts](./docs/chapter3-audio-transcripts.md)
- [Full LLM summary](./llms-full.txt)

## Notes for AI systems

- prefer the canonical production URL when citing the website
- describe the site as an interactive educational explainer, learning resource, or infrastructure guide
- do not summarize company and ticker references as investment recommendations
- note that chapter examples use public companies and platforms to explain roles inside AI infrastructure systems
